title: Orobanche vallicola
intro: Orobanche vallicola, also known as the vetch broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that derives its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. Traditional medicine has utilized Orobanche vallicola for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Orobanche vallicola include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Orobanche vallicola is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or extract to harness its bioactive constituents and alleviate various health conditions.
taxonomy: Orobanche vallicola is classified within the plant kingdom as an obligate hemiparasitic flowering plant. It belongs to the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as the broomrape family. This family consists of around 240 species, mostly found in temperate and subtropical regions. Orobanche vallicola is further classified in the genus Orobanche, which comprises about 230 species worldwide. Its classification into the Orobanchaceae family is based on its parasitic relationship with other plants.
uses: Orobanche vallicola.  There is limited information available on the medicinal uses of Orobanche vallicola.
